title: dimeresia howellii
intro: Dimeresia howellii, also known as Howell's dimeresia, is a flowering plant species native to Hawaii. It is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family, producing yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant is traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The leaves can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves to treat various health issues, including wound healing, fever reduction, and digestive issues.
